Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Cnidaria; Anthozoa; Hexacorallia; Corallimorpharia; Discosomidae; Rhodactis
Assembly: The assembly jaRhoOscu1.1 is based on 69x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Aquatic Symbiosis Genomics Project (https://www.aquaticsymbiosisgenomics.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 2 (16,394 - 19,092 Kbp) are unknown.The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 3 (10,000 - 12,000 Kbp) are unknown.The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 8 (11,500 - 12,345 Kbp) are unknown.The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 10 (1,242 - 2,106 & 9,931-11,102 & 16,322 - 16,737 Kbp) are unknown.The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 16 6,178 - 6,983 Kbp) are unknown.

Assembly: The assembly jaPalCari2.1 is based on 98x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Aquatic Symbiosis Genomics Project (https://www.aquaticsymbiosisgenomics.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. A large number of centromeric repeats is in the unlocalised sequences.

Assembly: The assembly ilIstMuri1.hap1.1 is based on 119x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Taxonomy_x: Eukaryota; Sar; Stramenopiles; Ochrophyta; Bacillariophyta; Fragilariophyceae; Fragilariophycidae; Licmophorales; Ulnariaceae; Tabularia
Description: Tabularia sp. RCC1743 genome assembly, chromosome: 12
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Sar; Stramenopiles; Ochrophyta; Bacillariophyta; Fragilariophyceae; Fragilariophycidae; Licmophorales; Ulnariaceae; Tabularia
Assembly: The assembly of Tabularia sp. RCC1743 (uoTabSpeb1) is based on 40X PacBio data and 61X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio+HiC assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS of each haplotype. In total, 138 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 14.062 Mb (with the largest being 1.615 Mb). Finally, Both haplotypes were analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. The mitochondrial and chloroplastic genomes were assembled using OATK. During manual curation, 20 haplotypic regions were removed, totaling 7.795Mb (with the largest being 1.781Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.
